comment_text.sn35.236:1.1: According to the doctrine of kamma, we acquire our bodies with their faculties from our desire. It does not just happen to be the case that we have hands to pick things up; we have manifested rebirth with hands because we want to pick things up.

root_text.sn35.236:1.5: Evameva kho, bhikkhave, cakkhusmiṁ sati cakkhusamphassapaccayā uppajjati ajjhattaṁ sukhaṁ dukkhaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn35.236:1.6: jivhāya sati jivhāsamphassapaccayā uppajjati ajjhattaṁ sukhaṁ dukkhaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn35.236:1.7: manasmiṁ sati manosamphassapaccayā uppajjati ajjhattaṁ sukhaṁ dukkhaṁ …pe….
root_text.sn35.236:2.1: Hatthesu, bhikkhave, asati ādānanikkhepanaṁ na paññāyati;
root_text.sn35.236:2.4: kucchismiṁ asati jighacchā pipāsā na paññāyati.
root_text.sn35.236:2.5: Evameva kho, bhikkhave, cakkhusmiṁ asati cakkhusamphassapaccayā nuppajjati ajjhattaṁ sukhaṁ dukkhaṁ …pe…
root_text.sn35.236:2.7: manasmiṁ asati manosamphassapaccayā nuppajjati ajjhattaṁ sukhaṁ dukkhan”ti.

translation_text.sn35.236:0.3: The Simile of Hands and Feet (1st)
translation_text.sn35.236:1.1: “Mendicants, when there are hands, picking up and putting down are found.
translation_text.sn35.236:1.2: When there are feet, coming and going are found.
translation_text.sn35.236:1.3: When there are joints, contracting and extending are found.
translation_text.sn35.236:1.4: When there’s a belly, hunger and thirst are found.
translation_text.sn35.236:1.5: In the same way, when there’s an eye, pleasure and pain arise internally conditioned by eye contact.
translation_text.sn35.236:1.6: When there’s an ear … nose … tongue … body …
translation_text.sn35.236:1.7: mind, pleasure and pain arise internally conditioned by mind contact.
translation_text.sn35.236:2.1: When there are no hands, picking up and putting down aren’t found.
translation_text.sn35.236:2.2: When there are no feet, coming and going aren’t found.
translation_text.sn35.236:2.3: When there are no joints, contracting and extending aren’t found.
translation_text.sn35.236:2.4: When there’s no belly, hunger and thirst aren’t found.
translation_text.sn35.236:2.5: In the same way, when there’s no eye, pleasure and pain don’t arise internally conditioned by eye contact.
translation_text.sn35.236:2.6: When there’s no ear … nose … tongue … body …
translation_text.sn35.236:2.7: mind, pleasure and pain don’t arise internally conditioned by mind contact.”
